name: Lead Belly
abstract: Huddie William Ledbetter (January 15, 1888 – December 6, 1949), better known by the stage name Lead Belly, was an American folk and blues singer notable for his strong vocals, virtuosity on the twelve-string guitar, and the folk standards he introduced, including his renditions of "In the Pines" (also known as "Where Did You Sleep Last N…

about_quotes.speaker: John E. Wood
about_quotes.evidence: I had particularly enjoyed some black folk blues people, Lead Belly. I had a great collection of Lead Belly records when I was a high school student.

about_quotes.speaker: Angola Talking
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Now, what about, uh... Lead belly? Did he ever do any of that type of work with his gang, you know? [INTERVIEWEE] Well, yes, sir. He did.

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Blumberg
about_quotes.evidence: I think...I know Lead Belly was there, and Danny Barker, and Simeon was there.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Lovett
about_quotes.evidence: I like Lead Belly and, uh, Leroy Carr and a lot of, a lot of people like that

about_quotes.speaker: John "Knocky" Parker
about_quotes.evidence: Uh, very much like, uh, Blind Lemon, and, uh, in a way, Lead Belly.

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: I must have heard him and I was really impressed by his approach
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
